purpose: Test hypothesis that NaN-guard plateaus cause NUTS divergence. Hypothesis falsified.
nan_guard_mode: hard (-1e10 log-density penalty when any predict_psa(θ) is NaN)

comparison.verdict: Posterior means are essentially identical between soft and hard NaN-guard runs (differences < 0.5%). This falsifies the NaN-plateau hypothesis: chains never reach NaN regions in steady state — they diverge during warmup before NaN matters.

interpretation: Posterior mean log(r_TP) ≈ log(15934) ≈ 9.68, prior mean = -5.37, prior std = 0.32.
9.68 - (-5.37) = 15.05 absolute distance ≈ 47 standard deviations under the prior.
Under a proper LogNormal prior, this region has log-density ≈ -1100 — astronomically unlikely.
Conclusion: NUTS is sampling from a chain that diverged during warmup, NOT from the actual posterior.
Fix requires: (a) deterministic init at canonical θ or MH posterior mean (not random), and/or
(b) different sampler with explicit mass-matrix initialization (Stan, variational warm-start).
